perturbation.original_text: Information" shall for the purpose of this agreement include, without limitation, any technical, commercial, scientific information, know -how, trade secrets, processes, machinery, designs, drawings, technical spe cifications, data in whatever form and information referred to in clause 1 (introduction) hereinabove communicated to the receiving party or acquired by the receiving party from the disclosing party during the course of the parties' association with one an other.
perturbation.changed_text: "Information" shall for the purpose of this agreement include, without limitation, any technical, commercial, scientific information, know -how, trade secrets, processes, machinery, designs, drawings, technical spe cifications, data in whatever form. Information shall exclude any information referred to in clause 1 (introduction) hereinabove communicated to the receiving party or acquired by the receiving party from the disclosing party during the course of the parties' association with one an other.
perturbation.explanation: By explicitly excluding information referenced in Clause 1 from the definition of 'Information' in Section 2, while Clause 3 onwards refers to ‘the information’ which now excludes Clause 1 introduction section. This creates an in-text contradiction. Earlier the ‘information’ reffered in clause 1 (introduction) was to be understood under the umbrella of definition of ‘Information’. But now the exclusion of this section, while the text later still refers to it, creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION 3.1 The parties agree to disclose the information to one another. 3.2 The parties acknowledge that the information is a valuable, special and unique 3 asset proprietary to the disclosing party. 3.3 The parties agree that they will no t, during the course of their association with one another or, thereafter, disclose the information to any third party for any reason or purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, save in accordance with the provisions of this agreement. 3.4 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained, in this agreement the parties agree that the information may be disclosed by the receiving party to its professional advisors, agents and consultants, for the purposes of evaluating the investigation and enter into an agreement as contemplated in clause 1 (introduction), providing that the receiving party takes whatever steps are necessary to procure that such professional advisors, agents and consultants agree to abide by the terms of th is agreement to prevent the unauthorised disclosure of the information to third parties. 3.5 The receiving party agrees: 3.5.1 not to utilise, employ, exploit or in any other manner whatsoever use the information disclosed pursuant to the provisions of this agreem ent for any purpose without the prior express written consent of the disclosing party. 3.5.2 that the unauthorised disclosure of the information to a third party may cause irreparable loss, harm and damage to the disclosing party. Accordingly, the receiving par ty indemnifies and holds the disclosing party harmless against any loss, action, claim, harm or damage, of whatever nature, suffered by the disclosing party pursuant to a breach by the receiving party of the provisions of this agreement,
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes information referenced in Clause 1 (introduction) from the definition of 'Information'. However, Clause 3.4 of the contradicted text refers to 'an agreement as contemplated in clause 1 (introduction)' which is now not considered part of the 'information' that can be disclosed. This creates a direct contradiction as the 'information' referred to in Clause 3.4 is now outside the defined scope, causing inconsistency and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party undertakes not to use the information fo r purpose other than: 5.2.1 that for which it is disclosed; and 5.2.2 in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party undertakes to use the information fo r any purpose, including but not limited to: 5.2.1 purposes other than that for which it is disclosed; and 5.2.2 uses that are not in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'not to use the information for purpose other than' to 'to use the information for any purpose,' the modified text directly contradicts the original restriction. This creates a contradiction because the original text intends to limit the receiving party's use of the information to only the purposes for which it was disclosed and in accordance with the agreement. However, the changed text states the opposite—that the receiving party can use the information for any purpose, even those outside the scope of the agreement and the purpose for which it was originally disclosed. This change undermines the confidentiality and non-disclosure aspects of the agreement. This contradicts section 6 - Standard of Care, creating uncertainty about what the ‘standard of care’ is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: STANDARD OF CARE The parties agree that they shall protect the information disclosed pursuant to the provisions of this agreement using the s ame standard of care that each party applies to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information and that the information shall be stored and handled in such a way as to prevent any unauthorised disclosure thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the receiving party can use the information for any purpose, which directly contradicts the 'STANDARD OF CARE' section. The 'STANDARD OF CARE' section requires the parties to protect the information with the same standard of care they apply to their own confidential information, preventing unauthorized disclosure. The changed text removes any restriction on the use of the information, even if it leads to unauthorized disclosure, thus contradicting the standard of care requirement.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be deemed to have commenced upon the date of last 6 signing hereof and it shall continue to bind the parti es for 3 (three) years after its termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be deemed to have commenced upon the date of last 6 signing hereof and it shall continue to bind the parti es indefinitely after its termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a duration of '3 (three) years after its termination.' The modified text changes this to 'indefinitely after its termination.' This is a direct contradiction, as one states a fixed term and the other suggests no end date. This introduces ambiguity and impacts Section 7 - Return of Information, where the term duration becomes relevant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN OF INFORMATION 7.1 The disclo sing party may at any time, request the receiving party to return any material containing, pertaining to or relating to information disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and may, in addition request the receiving party to furnish a written stat ement to the effect that, upon such return, the receiving party has not retained in possession, or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such material. 7.2 As an alternative to the return of the material contemplated in 7.1 above, the receivin g party shall, at the instance of the disclosing party, destroy such material and furnish the disclosing party with a written statement to the effect that all such material has been destroyed. 5 7.3 The receiving party shall comply with a request, in terms of t his clause 7, within 7 days of receipt of such a request,
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the binding period to 3 years after termination, while the changed text extends this indefinitely. This directly contradicts the Return of Information clause (Section 7), as the duration for which the receiving party must comply with requests to return or destroy information becomes uncertain. The fixed term in the original text provided a clear endpoint for these obligations, but the indefinite term in the changed text removes this clarity, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be deemed to have become terminated in the event of the following (whichever happens the sooner): 9.2.1 the discussions between the parties being unsuccessful and accordingly breaking down; or 9.2.2 an agreement between the parties being concluded regarding their future relationship; or 9.2.3 in any event not later than one year after signing hereof unless extended by mutual agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be deemed to have become terminated in the event of the following (whichever happens the sooner): 9.2.1 the discussions between the parties being unsuccessful and accordingly breaking down; or 9.2.2 an agreement between the parties being concluded regarding their future relationship; or 9.2.3 in any event not later than one year after signing hereof unless extended by mutual agreement. This agreement shall *not* be terminated under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'This agreement shall *not* be terminated under any circumstances,' the text creates a contradiction. The preceding clauses (9.2.1, 9.2.2, 9.2.3) outline specific conditions under which the agreement *will* terminate. The added sentence negates all those conditions, creating a direct conflict about the termination of the agreement, and affecting clause 9.3
perturbation.contradicted_text: This clause will survive the cancellation of this agreement for what ever reason.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the clause 'This agreement shall *not* be terminated under any circumstances.' This directly contradicts the original clause 9.2.1, 9.2.2, and 9.2.3 which outline specific scenarios for termination. The contradicted text states 'This clause will survive the cancellation of this agreement for what ever reason.' which confirms that the agreement can be cancelled under circumstances that are now directly denied by the added clause in the changed text, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute arising out of or in connection with the provisions of this agreement shall be referred to arbitration and determined in accordance with the Arbitration Act 42 of 1965, as amended from time to time,
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute arising out of or in connection with the provisions of this agreement shall be referred to mediation. If mediation fails, the parties shall resolve the dispute through public court litigation, waiving any rights to confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates arbitration, keeping the proceedings private. The modified text requires mediation first, and if that fails, switches to public court litigation. This contradicts the initial arbitration clause by removing that avenue for dispute resolution and forcing disputes into the public domain and contradicts clause 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: STANDARD OF CARE The parties agree that they shall protect the information disclosed pursuant to the provisions of this agreement using the s ame standard of care that each party applies to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information and that the information shall be stored and handled in such a way as to prevent any unauthorised disclosure thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates arbitration which implies confidentiality. The changed text introduces public court litigation, which explicitly waives any rights to confidentiality, contradicting the STANDARD OF CARE clause that requires parties to protect confidential information. The change directly conflicts with the pre-existing obligation to maintain confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: No amendment, interpretation or waiver of any of the provisions of this agreement shall be effective unless reduced writing and signed by both the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Any amendment, interpretation, or waiver of any provision of this agreement may be conducted orally and enforced without documentation of any kind.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires any changes to be in writing and signed by both parties. The modified text states that changes can be oral and without documentation. This creates a direct contradiction regarding how amendments are made and enforced, rendering the enforceability of the initial agreement uncertain and making clause 16 contradict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ENTIRE AGREEMENT This agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties with respect to the subject matter of this agreement and supersedes all prior agreements between the parties, whether written or oral with respect to the subject matter of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The changed text allows for oral modifications and waivers, whereas the contradicted text (ENTIRE AGREEMENT) states the agreement is the entire agreement and supersedes all prior agreements, whether written or oral. This creates a direct conflict as oral modifications are now permissible, but the entire agreement clause asserts that the written agreement is comprehensive. The change makes oral agreements enforceable in a contract that specifies that the written document represents the entirety of the agreement. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws where contradictory clauses are placed in multiple locations creating uncertainity.
